Off Calendar Dec-05-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Francis Shehadeh, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: Ron Hardin
Jennifer L.Sommer, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Eric Neplokh

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiff Ron Hardin's Motion To Quash Defendant Kent's Deposition Subpoenas For Business Records as to subpoenas issued to Kaiser Permanente-People Solutions and California Pacific Medical Center-Human Resources is granted, except for records related to wage, hour, and attendance records. The motion is granted as to the subpoena issued to City College of San Francisco, except as to registration and attendance records. The court finds that the subpoenas are overly broad and invade Plaintiffs' right to privacy without good cause.

MODIFICATION: Wage and attendance includes W-4 form.

Order signed in open court.
